label: Plausible
description: Pull privacy-friendly web analytics data from Plausible using your site's stats API.

form.title: Connect Plausible
form.subtitle: Paste your Plausible API key and site ID to start pulling web analytics data.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste an API key from your Plausible account settings along with the site domain. No OAuth setup required — the simplest and fastest path.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Plausible API Key

### Steps

1. Log in to your Plausible account at [plausible.io](https://plausible.io).
2. Click your avatar or initials in the top-right corner and open **Settings**.
3. In the left sidebar, select **API Keys**.
4. Click **+ New API Key**, give it a name like `Anton`, and click **Create API Key**.
5. Copy the key shown — it is only displayed once. Paste it into the **API Key** field below.
6. Find your **Site ID**: it is the domain you registered in Plausible (e.g. `yoursite.com`). You can see it on the main dashboard or under **Sites** in your account. Paste it into the **Site ID** field below.

### Notes

- The API key is shown only once at creation. If you lose it, delete it and generate a new one from the same **API Keys** page.
- If you are on a self-hosted Plausible instance, enter your custom base URL in the **Base URL** field instead of the default.
- Plausible's Stats API is read-only, so this key can only retrieve data — it cannot modify your site settings.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Generated in Plausible → Settings → API Keys. Shown only once at creation.

fields.name: site_id
fields.label: Site ID
fields.description: The domain you registered in Plausible (e.g. yoursite.com). Visible on your Plausible dashboard.

fields.name: base_url
fields.label: Base URL
fields.description: Only change this if you are running a self-hosted Plausible instance. Leave blank to use the default cloud endpoint.
